 48→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 49→            ),
 50→        visitorModifiers =
 51→            setOf(
 52→                RunAfterRule(ARGUMENT_LIST_WRAPPING_RULE_ID, REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED),
 53→            ),
 54→    ),
 55→    Rule.Experimental {
 56→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 57→    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 58→
 59→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 60→        indentConfig =
 61→            IndentConfig(
 62→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 63→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 64→            )
 65→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 66→    }
 67→
 68→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 69→        node: ASTNode,
 70→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 71→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 72→    ) {
 73→        when (node.elementType) {
 74→            BINARY_EXPRESSION -> visitExpression(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 75→        }
 76→    }
 77→
 78→    private fun visitExpression(
 79→        node: ASTNode,
 80→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 81→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 82→    ) {
 83→        require(node.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION)
 84→
 85→        // First check whether the entire expression has to be pushed to the next line after and assignment in a property or function
 86→        node
 87→            .takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType.anyOf(PROPERTY, FUN) }
 88→            ?.takeIf { binaryExpression ->
 89→                binaryExpression
 90→                    .prevSibling { it.elementType == EQ }
 91→                    ?.let { noNewLineInClosedRange(it, binaryExpression.firstChildLeafOrSelf()) }
 92→                    ?: false
 93→            }?.takeIf { it.isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength() }
 94→            ?.let { expression ->
 95→                emit(
 96→                    expression.startOffset,
 97→                    "Line is exceeding max line length. Break line between assignment and expression",
 98→                    true,
 99→                )
100→                if (autoCorrect) {
101→                    expression.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expression.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent))
102→                }
103→            }
104→
105→        node
106→            .findChildByType(OPERATION_REFERENCE)
107→            ?.let { operationReference -> visitOperationReference(operationReference, emit, autoCorrect) }
108→    }
109→
110→    private fun visitOperationReference(
111→        node: ASTNode,
112→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
113→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
114→    ) {
115→        node
116→            .takeIf { it.elementType == OPERATION_REFERENCE }
117→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION }
118→            ?.takeIf { binaryExpression ->
119→                // Ignore binary expression inside raw string literals. Raw string literals are allowed to exceed max-line-length. Wrapping
120→                // (each) binary expression inside such a literal seems to create more chaos than it resolves.
121→                binaryExpression.parent { it.elementType == LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY } == null
122→            }?.takeIf { it.isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength() }
123→            ?.let { operationReference ->
124→                val isElvisOperator = operationReference.firstChildNode.elementType == ELVIS
125→                if (node.isCallExpressionFollowedByLambdaArgument() ||
126→                    (!isElvisOperator && cannotBeWrappedAtOperationReference(operationReference)) ||
127→                    (isElvisOperator && operationReference.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline())
128→                ) {
129→                    // Wrapping after operation reference might not be the best place in case of a call expression or just won't work as
130→                    // the left hand side still does not fit on a single line
131→                    emit(operationReference.startOffset, "Line is exceeding max line length", false)
132→                } else if (isElvisOperator) {
133→                    emit(
134→                        operationReference.startOffset,
135→                        "Line is exceeding max line length. Break line before operator in binary expression",
136→                        true,
137→                    )
138→                    if (autoCorrect) {
139→                        operationReference.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(node.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent))
140→                    }
141→                } else {
142→                    operationReference
143→                        .nextSibling()
144→                        ?.let { nextSibling ->
145→                            emit(
146→                                nextSibling.startOffset,
147→                                "Line is exceeding max line length. Break line after operator in binary expression",
148→                                true,
149→                            )
150→                            if (autoCorrect) {
151→                                nextSibling.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(operationReference.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent))
152→                            }
153→                        }
154→                }
155→            }
156→    }
157→
158→    private fun ASTNode.isCallExpressionFollowedByLambdaArgument() =
159→        parent { it.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST }
160→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION }
161→            ?.nextCodeSibling()
162→            .let { it?.elementType == LAMBDA_ARGUMENT }
163→
164→    private fun cannotBeWrappedAtOperationReference(operationReference: ASTNode) =
165→        operationReference
166→            .takeUnless { it.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION }
167→            ?.let {
168→                val stopAtOperationReferenceLeaf = operationReference.firstChildLeafOrSelf()
169→                maxLineLength <=
170→                    it
171→                        .leavesOnLine()
172→                        .takeWhile { leaf -> leaf != stopAtOperationReferenceLeaf }
173→                        .lengthWithoutNewlinePrefix()
174→            }
175→            ?: false
176→
177→    private fun ASTNode.isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength() = leavesOnLine().lengthWithoutNewlinePrefix() > maxLineLength
178→
179→    private fun Sequence<ASTNode>.lengthWithoutNewlinePrefix() =
180→        joinToString(separator = "") { it.text }
181→            .dropWhile { it == '\n' }
182→            .length
183→}
184→
185→private fun IElementType.anyOf(vararg elementType: IElementType): Boolean = elementType.contains(this)
186→
187→public val BINARY_EXPRESSION_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = BinaryExpressionWrappingRule().ruleId
188→
